page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing two threads per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory bandwidth and text processing. The instance achieves a strong rating in uncached large-block memory read (208.51 GB/sec) and write (99.59 GB/sec) bandwidth, alongside strong performance in static web serving throughput at 44.59 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference text generation for small 135M models, reaching 328.98 tokens/sec. Other benchmarks, such as Geekbench multi-core (15821.0) and Passmark CPU (38513.51), fall within the average tier.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU and dedicated Intel compute resources, the n4-highmem-32 offers qualitative resource density advantages for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. The presence of advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) supports specialized acceleration for vector and matrix operations. This makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM on an Intel x86_64 architecture. Built with 16 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache, it delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but excels in memory-intensive tasks. Benchmark results show strong performance in uncached large-block memory bandwidth, static web serving throughput, and top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models. Supported by Intel AVX-512 and AMX extensions, it provides efficient processing for vector and matrix operations. This profile makes the n4-highmem-32 highly suitable for database operations, memory-intensive caching, and enterprise workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute power.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, ideal for database operations and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, delivering strong memory bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel processors and advanced matrix extensions for memory-bound enterprise workloads.

Intel x86_64 architecture with AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions
Strong uncached memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks
Top-tier text generation performance for small-scale LLM inference
Optimized for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive workloads
